questions.question: What distinguishes verification, identification, and open-set speaker recognition?
options: They are three names for the same task
Verification is 1:1 (is this the claimed person?), identification is 1:N (which enrolled speaker?), open-set adds the option that it's an unknown speaker not in the bank
Verification requires video input
Identification only works on passphrases
questions.explanation: Same math underneath — embeddings plus cosine — but the task framing changes thresholds, evaluation, and what an error costs.

questions.question: What is EER and why does the field standardize on it?
options: Energy Efficiency Rating of the GPU
Equal Error Rate — the threshold point where False Accept Rate equals False Reject Rate; one number summarizing the trade-off, used in every paper, leaderboard, and procurement call
Encoder Error Residual from training
The average of WER and CER
questions.explanation: Production systems rarely operate AT the EER threshold (a bank weights false accepts heavier), but the crossover gives a single comparable number across models.

questions.question: What is the enrollment-verification pipeline?
options: Transcribe both clips and compare text
Enroll with 5-30 seconds of the target speaker → fixed-dimension embedding (192-d ECAPA-TDNN, 256-d WavLM-large); verify by embedding the test utterance, computing cosine similarity, and thresholding
Fine-tune a model per enrolled user
Compare raw waveforms sample by sample
questions.explanation: ECAPA-TDNN (squeeze-excitation 1D convs + attentive pooling, trained on VoxCeleb with AAM-softmax) has dominated since 2020: 0.87% EER at just 15M params.

questions.question: What does AAM-softmax (additive angular margin) do for speaker embeddings?
options: Speeds up training convergence only
Adds a margin m in angular space — cos(θ + m) for the correct class — forcing inter-class angular separation so embeddings of different speakers spread apart (typical m=0.2, scale s=30)
Removes the need for labels
Normalizes audio volume
questions.explanation: Cosine scoring only works if training explicitly shaped the angular geometry. Score normalization (S-norm/AS-norm against an imposter cohort) is the other essential for cross-domain evaluation.

questions.question: What is speaker diarization and its modern pipeline?
options: Removing background music from a clip
"Who spoke when" in multi-speaker audio: VAD → segment → embed each segment → cluster (agglomerative/spectral) → smooth boundaries — pyannote.audio 3.1 bundles it all; 2026 SOTA DER on AMI is ~15%
Converting speech to a diary entry
Detecting whether audio is synthetic
questions.explanation: Diarization composes the verification primitives: the same embeddings that answer "is this Alice?" get clustered to answer "who spoke when" — down from 23% DER in 2022.
